title: HTML Foundations
description: Learn semantic page structure, headings, links, forms, media, and accessibility-friendly markup.
whyItMatters: HTML is the skeleton of every website. Understanding structure makes every other frontend skill easier and helps you build pages that search engines, browsers, and users can understand.

title: Modern CSS Systems
description: Study layout, spacing, responsive design, Flexbox, Grid, transitions, and design tokens.
whyItMatters: CSS is what turns raw content into polished products. Good styling skills help you create interfaces that feel trustworthy, readable, and professional on every screen size.

title: JavaScript Interactivity
description: Practice variables, arrays, objects, functions, DOM updates, events, async code, and fetch requests.
whyItMatters: JavaScript powers interaction on the web. It lets users search, filter, submit forms, load data, and work with real-time interfaces instead of static pages.

title: Vue.js Components
description: Build reusable components, page routing, reactive state, forms, and structured frontends with Vue 3.
whyItMatters: Vue helps developers organize growing interfaces into clean reusable pieces. That makes projects easier to scale, maintain, and present in professional portfolios.

title: Backend APIs with Express
description: Create routes, accept JSON requests, validate data, and return responses that a frontend can use.
whyItMatters: Backend APIs connect the interface to real data. They are the reason apps can save profiles, dashboards, posts, and business logic instead of only showing temporary information.

title: Database Thinking
description: Understand persistent storage, records, IDs, validation, and how applications read and write structured data.
whyItMatters: Data storage is what makes an app useful after refresh. Even a simple JSON file demonstrates the core database idea: your app should remember what users create and edit.

title: Deployment and Launch
description: Learn how to move a project from local development to a live environment with build steps, hosting, and environment configuration.
whyItMatters: Deployment is how your work reaches real users, teachers, clients, or hiring managers. A finished app only becomes truly useful once people can access it online.
